title: Maya doesnt have to put up with this crap Mayas a cyborg
display_title: Maya doesnt have to put up with this crap Mayas a cyborg
wikitext:
[]: Maya doesn't have to put up with this crap! Maya's a cyborg!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Sep 03, 2009 9:50 pm I recently read this really negative review of EoE that says: I have seen this movie several times, and still cannot enjoy the film one iota. I do not believe that it is because I didn't "get" the film; I believe that there is quite simply nothing to get. Certain Evangelion fanboys have gotten a huge kick out of analyzing the show and attempting to fabricate some sort of interpretation of the events that transpire, but in my opinion this is a futile endeavor. The shows plot was deliberately made incomprehensible for incomprehensibilitys sake; there is no meaning to the show other than to have no meaning whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Thu Sep 03, 2009 9:56 pm Her hand splattered on the computer screen when she got tanged. There weren't any robo parts in it. As for the other hand I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Sep 03, 2009 10:06 pm Sammaeloo wrote: Her hand splattered on the computer screen when she got tanged. There weren't any robo parts in it. As for the other hand I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 03, 2009 11:34 pm I say lol Anno. Love the thread title btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Sep 04, 2009 2:24 am Sammaeloo wrote: Her hand splattered on the computer screen when she got tanged. There weren't any robo parts in it. As for the other hand I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Sep 04, 2009 4:40 am Maybe she lacked a (complete) hand for whatever reason (accident, birth anomaly) and got a prosthesic one. A fully functional and aesthetically realistic one at that. Wow.

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Fri Sep 04, 2009 6:09 am Hehe, well--- actually, I'd always thought there was a "hand-exploding" sound at the moment her hand splatters across the monitor - just as it cuts to Seele's... lair. Then again, I'm most likely completely wrong (EDIT: I just check, and indeed, I am wrong- oh well!). Either way, I suppose it just sort of works. For me, at least. Love the thread title btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Sep 04, 2009 8:04 am LiLi wrote: A fully functional and aesthetically realistic one at that. Wow.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Fri Sep 04, 2009 8:31 am It's lol Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Sep 04, 2009 9:18 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: LiLi wrote: A fully functional and aesthetically realistic one at that. Wow.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Fri Sep 04, 2009 9:24 am I think it was just a way to get both what was on screen as well as showing maya tanging at the same time. In any case, I'm pretty certain that those hands are 100% LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Sep 04, 2009 9:59 am Zaque wrote: I think it was just a way to get both what was on screen as well as showing maya tanging at the same time. In any case, I'm pretty certain that those hands are 100% LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Fri Sep 04, 2009 12:57 pm Exactly. It's baseless speculation. The only character who's shown with any kind of prosthetic is Keel, and it can be concluded that what he has is secret technology only available to a small group, if not only him.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Sep 04, 2009 5:09 pm sean_sean_sean_sean wrote: To be honest, I sort of thought the OP was joking around, but perhaps he wasn't. I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 04, 2009 5:35 pm No i don't think Maya's was a cyborg. I amazed how new stuff like this comes up every so often.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Sep 04, 2009 5:36 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: You shouldn't be to surprised. This is the [fictional] world of 2015.

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Fri Sep 04, 2009 6:43 pm Sigh.... You new people don't know NAW yet.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Fri Sep 04, 2009 7:59 pm sean_sean_sean_sean wrote: Hehe, well--- actually, I'd always thought there was a "hand-exploding" sound at the moment her hand splatters across the monitor - just as it cuts to Seele's... lair. Then again, I'm most likely completely wrong (EDIT: I just check, and indeed, I am wrong- oh well!). Either way, I suppose it just sort of works. For me, at least. Love the thread title btw.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 05, 2009 3:00 pm DatDude wrote: No i don't think Mays was a cyborg.

[]:             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Sat Sep 05, 2009 3:00 pm lolAnno. But once again; Maya's fingers are under scrutiny. Perhaps there is some super important connection between Maya and hands/fingers in Evangelion that we just arent seeing~
